edges.description: Persistent HPV infection enables sustained E6 expression
edges.source: HPV Infection of Anal Squamous Epithelium

edges.description: Persistent HPV infection enables sustained E7 expression
edges.source: HPV Infection of Anal Squamous Epithelium

edges.description: p53 loss permits cells with damaged DNA to survive and proliferate
Unrestrained proliferation in the setting of impaired checkpoints
Genomic alterations drive progression from AIN to invasive carcinoma
Invasive HPV-driven anal squamous cell carcinoma upregulates PD-L1 and engages adaptive immune resistance to evade anti-tumor T cell responses.
HPV-driven dysplasia progression culminates in invasive anal canal squamous carcinoma, the defining neoplastic phenotype.
Invasive anal canal squamous carcinoma commonly presents with bleeding.
Anal canal cancer can produce anal or perirectal pain as a local symptom.
Primary tumor progression can involve inguinal nodes, producing inguinal lymphadenopathy.
intermediate_mechanisms: regional lymphatic metastasis to inguinal nodes

nodes.description: Anal squamous cell carcinoma cells frequently express PD-L1 and PD-1 is expressed on tumor-infiltrating T cells, consistent with adaptive immune resistance to anti-tumor immunity. This mechanism is the therapeutic rationale for PD-1 blockade (pembrolizumab, nivolumab) in recurrent or metastatic disease.

biological_processes: Negative Regulation of T Cell Mediated Immunity
cell_types: stratified squamous epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization of squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal.
evidence.snippet: The expression of PD-1 was seen in 68.8% and PD-L1 in 40.5% of tumors.

nodes.description: Invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal is the defining histopathologic feature of HPV-associated anal canal cancer.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization of squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal.
evidence.snippet: Squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal (SCCA) is an uncommon malignancy with limited therapeutic options.

nodes.description: HPV-driven dysplasia of the anal squamous epithelium progresses through a spectrum of anal intraepithelial neoplasia (AIN1, AIN2, AIN3 / high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion) to invasive anal squamous cell carcinoma. High-grade lesions carry a significant risk of progression, particularly in immunosuppressed patients.

cell_types: stratified squamous epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: Recent Guidelines on Anal Cancer Screening: A Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: the benefits of treating precancerous anal lesions to reduce the risk of progression to anal SCC

nodes.description: Pain is among the multiple symptoms commonly experienced by patients with anal canal cancer over the course of their disease, as documented in palliative care studies of colorectal and anal malignancies.

evidence.reference_title: Palliative care in colorectal and anal malignancies from diagnosis to death.
evidence.snippet: Patients diagnosed with both cancers often experience multiple symptoms including pain, constipation, nausea, and vomiting.

nodes.description: The HPV E6 oncoprotein binds to the cellular E3 ubiquitin ligase E6AP and targets p53 for proteasomal degradation. Loss of p53 function eliminates DNA damage checkpoints and apoptotic responses in infected anal squamous epithelial cells.

cell_types: stratified squamous epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: The E6 oncoprotein encoded by human papillomavirus types 16 and 18 promotes the degradation of p53.
evidence.snippet: the E6 proteins of the oncogenic HPVs

evidence.reference_title: The E6 oncoprotein encoded by human papillomavirus types 16 and 18 promotes the degradation of p53.
evidence.snippet: p53 is ATP dependent and involves the ubiquitin-dependent protease system.

nodes.description: The HPV E7 oncoprotein binds to and inactivates the retinoblastoma protein (pRB), releasing E2F transcription factors and driving uncontrolled cell cycle progression in anal squamous epithelial cells.

biological_processes: G1/S transition of mitotic cell cycle
cell_types: stratified squamous epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: The human papilloma virus-16 E7 oncoprotein is able to bind to the retinoblastoma gene product.
evidence.snippet: the E7 oncoprotein of the human papilloma virus type-16 can

nodes.description: Combined loss of p53 and pRB function, along with HPV-associated chromosomal aberrations, drives accumulation of genetic alterations and progression from anal intraepithelial neoplasia to invasive squamous cell carcinoma.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular characterization of squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal.
evidence.snippet: The most frequently mutated genes included PIK3CA (28.1%), KMT2D (19.5%), FBXW7 (12%), TP53 (12%) and PTEN (10.8%).

nodes.description: High-risk HPV (predominantly HPV-16) infects basal stratified squamous epithelial cells of the anal canal, typically at the anal transformation zone where squamous and columnar epithelia meet. Persistent infection over years to decades is the necessary first step in HPV-driven anal carcinogenesis.
nodes.id: HPV Infection of Anal Squamous Epithelium

cell_types: stratified squamous epithelial cell of anal canal

evidence.reference_title: Cancer epidemiology in rare and hereditary colorectal diseases 2) anal canal cancer (cancer statistics).
evidence.snippet: HPV was positive in 85% of the SCC cases, with HPV-16 as the most prevalent genotype

nodes.description: PD-1 inhibitors such as pembrolizumab and nivolumab have demonstrated activity in recurrent or metastatic anal canal squamous cell carcinoma and are used in the second-line and beyond setting.

evidence.reference_title: Pembrolizumab for previously treated advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma: results from the non-randomised, multicohort, multicentre, phase 2 KEYNOTE-158 study.
evidence.snippet: Pembrolizumab monotherapy is a possible treatment option with a favourable benefit-risk ratio for patients with previously treated advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma who have no alternative satisfactory treatment options.

nodes.description: Anal canal cancer below the dentate line drains preferentially to inguinal lymph nodes; palpable inguinal lymphadenopathy may indicate regional metastatic involvement.

evidence.reference_title: Metastatic Status and Dissection Effect of Regional/Extraregional Lymph Nodes in Japanese Patients with Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Anal Canal: A Multicenter Retrospective Cohort Study.
evidence.snippet: Primary tumor progression was associated with metastasis and recurrence of the inguinal node

nodes.description: Bright red bleeding per rectum is the most common presenting symptom of anal canal cancer and frequently prompts initial evaluation.

evidence.snippet: Hematochezia is the passage of frank blood per rectum

evidence.reference_title: Squamous cell carcinoma of the anal canal.
evidence.snippet: common presenting symptom is bleeding, with up to one third of patients
